title: paspalum azuayense
intro: Paspalum azuayense, also known as palo de fraile, is a type of perennial grass native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It has been found to possess medicinal properties, particularly its leaves, which are used to treat various health issues due to their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Paspalum azuayense include its ability to alleviate symptoms of fever, diarrhea, and respiratory infections, as well as its use as a natural remedy for skin conditions and wounds. The herbal preparations of Paspalum azuayense typically involve boiling the leaves in water to create a tea or infusing them in oil to make a topical ointment for wound care and skin treatment.
